turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the court this morning, Case 23-1038, Food and Drug Administration v. Wages and White Lion Investments.

turns.name: JUSTICE ALITO
turns.text: Invoking authority granted by the Family Smoking Prevention and Tobacco Control Act of 2009, the Food and Drug Administration denied thousands of applications to market flavored e-cigarette products. In this case, disappointed applicants challenged the FDA's actions under the Administrative Procedure Act. The main thrust of their argument is that the FDA changed the position it had taken in guidance provided before they submitted their applications. They argue that the denial of their applications was therefore arbitrary and capricious. Based on the FDA's guidance, regulated parties may have come away with the impression that the FDA would apply less demanding standard of proof than is evident in the denial orders it ultimately issued, but arbitrary and capricious review gives agencies a lot of leeway and in the end, we cannot say that the applicants have made the requisite showing. As the government effectively concedes however, the FDA erred in refusing to take into account the applicant's marketing plans and we decline to pursue the government's request that we hold that the error was harmless. We leave that question to be decided on remand. The judgment of the Court of Appeals is vacated and the case is remanded for further proceedings consistent with this opinion. The opinion of the court is unanimous. Justice Sotomayor has filed a concurring opinion.
